checks.a_analysis: The output JSON consists solely of information directly extracted from the source PDF. The headings, paragraphs, and list items in the JSON correspond to the 'Work Plan', 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee', 'Goals', 'Objectives', 'Expected Benefits', 'Ongoing Responsibilities', and 'Major Tasks' sections of the document. No fabricated text was found.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs), and neither does the JSON output.

All three pages of the source document are represented in the JSON output. Page 1's narrative and lists are included (Reading Order 1-13). Page 2's major tasks table (tasks 2.1 to 4.3) is included (Reading Order 17-20). Page 3's major tasks table (tasks 4.4 to 4.10) is included (Reading Order 24-27).

The table spanning pages 2 and 3 has a total of 15 data rows in the source document (Tasks 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 3.1, 3.2, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8, 4.9, 4.10). The JSON output represents these in two tables (splitting by page) with 8 and 7 data rows respectively, totaling 15 data rows. No rows were omitted.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core content within each section is fully preserved. While the headers (NC GICC, FY 2019-2020) and footers (Page X of 3) appearing at the top/bottom of each page in the source are not repeated as elements within every section of the JSON, this is standard practice for structured documents where such repeating elements are typically treated as metadata rather than body content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content matches the source document exactly. Lists that were numbered or lettered in the source (Goals A-C, Objectives 1-4, Benefits 1-4) are converted to unordered lists in the JSON structure with the markers removed from the string content. This is acceptable semantic reformatting as the list order and text integrity are maintained.

checks.a_analysis: The output contains repeated headers 'Work Plan' and 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee' at the start of each section, which corresponds to the header block present on every page of the source document. Per the acceptable changes list, intentional repetition in the source (like page headers) does not constitute a defect. No actual content paragraphs or list items are duplicated.

The heading hierarchy is consistent and logical. H1 is used for the main title, and H2 for major sections like 'Goals', 'Objectives', etc. In Section 2, 'Major Tasks...' is correctly nested as an H3 under the committee's H2. There are no skips in hierarchy levels within sections.

The document contains a table split across two pages in the source. The JSON correctly identifies this as two distinct table elements (Section 2, reading_order 20 and Section 3, reading_order 26), preserving the column headers ('Objective number and task number', 'Lead Member', etc.) and the row structure for each part. Columns and data mapping are accurate to the source layout.

The reading order follows the top-to-bottom, page-by-page flow of the original document. The information is presented logically, starting with the committee's purpose and goals, followed by objectives and the specific task list table.

Several lists on the first page of the source use alphanumeric markers (e.g., A, B, C for 'Goals'; 1, 2, 3, 4 for 'Objectives'). In the JSON output, these have been converted to 'unordered' lists, and the text markers themselves (like 'A.') have been stripped from the content of the list items. This loses the specific numbering/lettering format intended in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Goals A. Geospatial datasets... B. Cooperative... C. Geospatial datasets... Objectives 1. Communicate... 2. Inform... 3. Promote... 4. Advise...
checks.b_evidence_output: The items in the 'Goals' and 'Objectives' lists are rendered with "list_type": "unordered" and the text content starts directly with the data, omitting the 'A.', 'B.', '1.', etc. markers.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any functional or clearly identifiable hyperlinks (e.g., .gov URLs or email addresses). The output correctly contains no link elements.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document correctly reflects the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-page PDF structure is translated into a continuous HTML page. Each page's header (NC GICC, Work Plan, etc.) is rendered as a heading at the start of its respective content section. The list structures (Goals, Objectives, etc.) and tables are preserved in their original order. The single-column layout is appropriate for the content.

The tables from pages 2 and 3 of the source PDF are correctly rendered as HTML tables. They include clear column headers, visible borders, and well-aligned cell content. The data is easily readable and the logical structure of rows and columns is maintained.
The source PDF contains no meaningful images, logos, or charts. It is a text-and-table-based document. Therefore, no images are missing or broken in the HTML output.

There is no duplication of body content (paragraphs, lists, or table data). While the document title 'Work Plan' and committee name 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee' appear multiple times, this is because they were page headers in the original PDF and have been converted to section headings in the single-page HTML output, which is an expected behavior for multi-page document conversion. No actual informational content is repeated redundantly within the body.
